Items.Title: GitHub 安全最佳实践——牢记 15 条提示 | GitHub Security Best Practices – 15 Tips To Keep In Mind
Items.Description:
是什么让 GitHub 脱颖而出？ 让我们看看……它是最大的源代码托管平台，有超过 1 亿的开发人员使用。 随着它的发展，它已经成为 DevOps 工作流程中不可或缺的一部分。 每个 CTO 或 DevSecOps 都明白忽视 Git 安全方面会导致严重后果。 数据泄露、财务损失、数据损坏、声誉和股价损失只是其中的一部分。

如果公司评估与使用 GitHub 相关的所有风险并采取预防措施来降低这些风险，它既可以保存其关键和敏感信息，又可以保持其业务连续性稳定。 在这篇博文中，我们将讨论如何构建 GitHub Advanced Security，但在此之前，让我们看看您的 GitHub Cloud Infrastructure 或 GitHub Enterprise 可能面临的风险。

是时候评估风险了……

为什么采取所有预防措施来保护您的 GitHub 环境很重要？ 让我们看看最近几年 GitHub 上的安全威胁轨迹，例如恶意软件和勒索软件活动：

  • 简而言之：我们可以说已经有成千上万的数据点、敏感和有价值的信息被盗、损坏或泄露，导致企业花费数千甚至数百万美元来克服这些问题。 看看我们对 2022 年最臭名昭著的 GitHub 相关安全事件（老实说，“fackups”）的终极回顾。

    试想一下，单个受害者对勒索软件的平均需求约为 57 万美元…… 这只是一笔赎金，其他费用呢，比如停机时间——最长可达 22 天（原文如此！）。 或者如果恶意行为者拒绝返回对您数据的访问权限怎么办？ 大约 92% 的支付赎金的人无法无损失地取回所有数据。 所以，有很多担忧……

    所有提到的统计数据和事实都很可怕，但有一种方法可以消除甚至克服这些麻烦的后果，从而节省精力和预算。

    提示 # 1 – 不要将您的凭据存储在 GitHub 上

    据 Dark Reading 称，2021 年企业泄露了超过 600 万个密码、API 密钥和其他敏感数据。 为什么？ 因为 DevOps 倾向于将他们所谓的“秘密”保存在他们的 GitHub 存储库中。 然后与他们的队友或整个公司分享他们的回购协议。

    因此，您应该始终注意的第一件事是您的GitHub 秘密。 为什么它如此重要？ 因为此类数据很容易被泄露，如果是这样，您不仅会丢失个人访问数据，还会丢失整个项目——数月甚至数年的辛勤工作和大量资金。

    幸运的是，GitHub 有预防措施来帮助您在这种情况下确保安全——GitHub 秘密扫描。 该服务会扫描 GitHub 存储库中所有分支上的整个 Git 历史记录，以查找泄露的登录凭据。 如果它注意到代码或提交元数据中的某些用户名或密码，它可以自动重置您的敏感信息并通过电子邮件通知您。 听起来不错

    合作伙伴模式秘密扫描，在公共存储库上自动运行。

  • 高级安全秘密扫描，这是对使用 GitHub Enterprise Cloud 的组织拥有的存储库的额外扫描。

还有第三方工具可以增强您的秘密检测能力，扫描您的代码以检测隐藏的密码、证书、API 或加密密钥，以及早发现漏洞并立即修复。 顺便提一下 GitGuardian 秘密扫描。

技巧 # 2 – 限制和控制授予的访问权限

正如我们已经提到的，数据泄露的主要原因之一是凭证泄露。 现在让我们从另一个角度来看这个。 这里的主要问题是谁授权访问您的敏感数据？

例如，您有一些临时开发人员加入您的 DevOps 团队只是短期，然后在他的工作完成并离开公司后，您的安全团队忘记限制他的访问。 那可能吗？ 当然......人为错误是数据丢失最常见的结果之一。

这就是为什么应该始终监控对密码详细信息的适当保护以及对授予访问权限的人的控制。

请记住可以保护您免受恶意访问和错误侵害的最小权限规则。

技巧 # 3 – 使用 GitHub 团队

您的 DevOps 团队中的每个人都应该对每个存储库具有相同的访问权限吗？ 当然不。 因此，为您组织的工作流程创建团队至关重要。 您可以根据组织的需要和要求创建开发人员、安全工程师和许多其他人的团队。 此外，您可以授予每个团队适当的访问权限，例如管理员、写入或仅读取。

技巧 # 4 – 使用 2FA 或 MFA

即使您有一个强大而可靠的密码——如您所想，它也可能会被泄露。 您可以使用 16 个以上的大小写、数字和符号的随机字符，但它仍然可以被破解。 是否可以加强密码？ 是的……使用双因素身份验证。

这种保护方式在您的登录过程中充当额外的安全保护层。 除了您提供的用于登录帐户的常规信息（用户名和密码）之外，您还需要提供一些只有您自己知道的额外数据，或者您可以使用您个人使用的设备来证明您的身份。 例如，您收到一条带有 2FA 代码的消息或您的手机接到一个电话。

顺便说一句，GitHub 强烈建议您启用双因素身份验证，并为您提供 2FA 恢复代码，以防您无法使用手机。 此外，GitHub 将要求所有用户在 2023 年底之前启用 2FA。

另一方面，对于更重要的信息和 N 倍更强的安全性，您可以启用多重身份验证。 在这种情况下，您添加了不止一项额外信息和/或仅由您所知和使用的设备。

技巧 # 5 – 轮换个人访问令牌和 SSH 密钥

您可能更愿意使用个人访问令牌或 SSH 密钥，并放弃使用“通用”密码对您的 GitHub 帐户进行身份验证。

不过，请不要忘记定期刷新它们是值得的。 另一个好主意是使用密码保护它或在硬件安全密钥上生成 SSH 密钥。 这将保护并减轻可能导致您的数据泄露或被盗的潜在损害。 就好像威胁行为者获得了对您的 SSH 密钥的访问权限，他将能够将恶意代码推送到您的存储库。

技巧 # 6 – 确保代码分支保护

当我们谈论某些规范或存储库中的所有分支时，它允许用户在存储库中为单个分支创建此类规则。 不过，值得注意的是，如果我们谈论您要指定的分支，您不应该忘记使用fnmatch语法。

你可能会问为什么我需要分支保护作为 git 安全措施？ 然而答案很简单——它一方面降低了内部威胁的风险，另一方面有助于避免将不需要的代码推入生产环境。

更准确地说，让我们看看 GitHub 建议采取的措施和安全功能：

  • 允许强制推送和删除
  • 限制那些可以推送到匹配分支的人的访问
  • 锁定分支
  • 在合并之前检查所需的拉取请求审查、状态检查和对话解决方案
  • 检查所需的签名提交
  • 需要线性历史和合并队列
  • 合并前要求部署成功

技巧 # 7 – 不要忘记创建一个 SECURITY.md 文件

为什么值得在您的开发过程中包含 SECURITY.md 文件？ 与 README.md 文件一样，SECURITY.md 文件包含有关您的项目的信息。 第一个只给出了项目的总体概述，第二个代表了在报告项目中的安全漏洞时应遵循的一组说明。 因此，它应该包括：

  • 披露政策，它定义了用户应该如何就发现的信息进行交流以及他们应该就该问题与谁联系的程序。
  • 安全更新策略，它定义了将发现的漏洞传达给其他人的过程。
  • 与安全相关的配置，其中包括有助于在项目部署期间加强安全态势的设置。
  • 未来的增强和已知的安全漏洞，其中包括您的组织尚未实施的一些安全改进。

技巧 # 8 – 禁用分叉

从开发的角度来看， git forking对于您的 DevOps 团队来说似乎是一个方便的选择，因为它允许您制作项目的副本并对其进行更改而不会影响主分支。 但是，从安全方面来看，它有其缺点。 首先，分叉会减慢安全跟踪的过程并使其变得更加复杂。

然后，fork 提供了将所有代码 repos 分叉给 DevOps 团队的每个成员的可能性。 如果它是您团队中的临时开发人员怎么办？ 如果他为了个人使用而分叉构建，会发生什么？ 您为之工作了很长时间并投入大量精力、努力和预算的团队建设？

技巧 # 9 – 三思而后行，要包含哪个 GitHub 应用程序

众所周知，GitHub 应用程序在开发过程中非常有用。 它可以简化 DevOps 构建和开发过程，但所有应用程序都安全吗？

当您选择任何应用程序作为功能添加到您的CI/CD 过程中时，注意是非常重要的。 您需要做的是通读所有关于软件安全性的评论，检查其生产商，并在授予对任何第三方应用程序的访问权限之前三思而后行。 您可以检查它的可信度，以及它是否采取了所有安全措施来保护您的数据。 例如，如果该第三方解决方案满足 SOC 2 或 ISO 27K 要求。

技巧 # 10 – 备份你的 GitHub 环境

定期备份有助于抵御各种不愉快的情况，例如 GitHub 中断或勒索软件攻击。 但是，为了确保您的数据安全并且可以从任何时间点轻松恢复，最好使用GitHub 备份最佳实践，包括：

  • 要备份的数据——确保它包括你所有的 GitHub 存储库和元数据
  • 存储选择——值得选择一些备份目的地来存储你的数据
  • 无限保留——保证您的项目将永远存储
  • 勒索软件保护——一组功能，可让您的 DevOps 团队相信源代码构建是安全的
  • 自动备份——可以在您的CI/CD 流程中实施。 作为首席技术官或安全负责人，您可以指派团队的一些成员定期编写脚本和执行备份——尽管这可能很耗时。 另一方面，您可以使用第三方软件，例如 GitProtect.io，它将负责数据备份并保证数据的可访问性和可恢复性。

技巧 #11 – 通过审计日志跟踪活动

如果组织的结构相当复杂且员工人数众多，则最好使用审计日志不时跟踪所有员工的活动。 审核日志包含有关执行操作的人员、执行此操作的时间以及操作内容的详细信息。

提示 # 12 – 定期扫描你的回购协议

定期扫描漏洞和错误可以为您和您的组织本身带来更多安宁，因为您的 DevOps 团队将有机会更快地检测和解决问题。 它将帮助开发人员防止产生新的安全问题并安心工作。

提示 # 13 – 确保您的私有存储库安全

如果您不需要在您的组织中使用公共存储库，那么值得禁用一个公共存储库的创建。 您可以在 GitHub 帐户中轻松完成此操作。 这很重要，因为它可以防止 DevOps 团队的任何成员意外创建可公开访问的存储库。 更重要的是，它减少了人为错误的可能性。

技巧 #14 – 管理 IP 地址

跟踪 DevOps 团队的操作非常重要，尽管这可能很困难。 作为一项安全措施，您可以限制对公司私有资产的访问。 最好的方法是什么？ 配置允许连接的 IP 地址列表。

使用此选项，您可以仅从办公室网络的 IP 地址授予访问权限。 如果您有一个远程团队，您可以使用 CIDR 表示法批准每个单独的 IP 访问。 因此，您将保证 IP 允许列表之外的地址通过 API、Web 或 Git 被阻止访问私有资源。

技巧 # 15 – 在将源代码导入 GitHub 之前检查源代码

在将代码导入 GitHub 之前，有一条规则可以对您的代码执行完整的审核，这很好。 这似乎是一个不重要的问题——审计一个小项目，但想象一下这个项目每天都在增长……你的 DevOps 团队需要多少时间来执行完整的审计、更新然后拉动它？ 几周甚至几个月。 因此，对较小部分的审核似乎是合理的。

带走

重要的是遵循简单的步骤来保护您的知识产权和资产，即源代码。 您可以使用 GitHub 提供的功能，但不要忘记 GitHub 是一项服务。 与任何其他 SaaS 一样，它根据共享责任模型运行。 这是什么意思？ 在共享责任模型下，源代码的安全是您自己的责任。

借助 GitHub 上提供的大量功能，请确保您使用所有必要的功能，以帮助您为 DevOps 团队的持续编码构建可靠的安全空间。



原文:

What makes GitHub stand out of others? Let’s see… It is the largest source code hosting platform, used by 100M+ developers. And with its growth it has become an integral part of the DevOps workflow. Every CTO or DevSecOps understands that neglecting the aspects of Git security can lead to serious consequences. Data breaches, financial loss, data corruption, reputation and stock price loss are just some of them.

If the company evaluates all the risks connected to using GitHub and takes preventive measures to mitigate those risks, it can both save its critical and sensitive information and keep its business continuity stable. In this blog post, we will speak about how to build GitHub Advanced Security, but before let’s look at the risks your GitHub Cloud Infrastructure or GitHub Enterprise can face.

It’s time to evaluate the risks…

Why is it important to take all the preventive measures to protect your GitHub environment? Let’s look at the track of the security threats, such as malware and ransomware activity on GitHub in the latest couple of years:

  • 2019 – A group of malware researchers from Trend Micro uncovered a watering hole campaign that involved the new variant of malware, abusing Slack and GitHub, consequently named as SLUB.
  • 2020 – The GitHub’s Security Incident Response Team issues the notice that some GitHub repositories using open-source Java projects are infected with the malicious code.
  • April 2022 – Attackers steal the login details of 100K npm accounts with the help of the stolen OAuth app tokens issued to Heroku and Travis-CI. (Read more about how NPM packages can be compromised here)
  • October 2022 – Checkmarx Supply Chain Security team notices a flaw that allows attackers to take control over repositories and infect codes and apps with malware.
  • November 2022 – Discloses a security breach after threat actors stole 130 code repos and gained access to one of its GitHub accounts using employee credentials stolen in a phishing attack.

In a nutshell: we can say that there have already been tons and thousands of data points, sensitive and valuable information stolen, corrupted or leaked causing enterprises to spend thousands or even millions of dollars to overcome those problems. Take a look at our Ultimate Review of the most infamous GitHub-related security incidents (let’s be honest, “fackups”) of 2022.

Just imagine, the average ransomware demand for a single victim makes up around $570K…. And it is just a ransom, what about other expenses, like time of downtime – which can last up to 22 days (sic!). Or what if the malicious actor refuses to return the access to your data? About 92% of those who paid the ransom were unable to get all their data back without a loss. So, there are a number of concerns…

GitHub Security Best Practices

All the mentioned statistics and facts are scary, but there is a way to eliminate those troublesome consequences or even overcome them, saving nerves and budget.

Tip # 1 – Don’t store your credentials on GitHub

According to Dark Reading in 2021 enterprises leaked over 6M passwords, API keys, and other sensitive data. Why? Because DevOps tend to keep their so-called “secrets” in their GitHub repository. And then share their repos with their teammates or the entire company.

Thus, the first thing you should always take care of is your GitHub secrets. Why is that so vital? Because such data can easily be compromised and if so you can lose not only your personal access data, but also the whole project – months or even years of hard work and loads of money.

To your luck, GitHub has preventive measures to help your security in this case – GitHub secret scanning. The service scans your entire Git history on all branches in your GitHub repository for leaked login credentials. If it notices some username or password in code or commit metadata, it can automatically reset your sensitive information and notify you via email. Sounds nice

GitHub provides secret scanning in two forms:

  • partner pattern secret scanning, which runs automatically on public repositories.
  • advanced security secret scanning, which is an additional scanning for repositories owned by the organizations using GitHub Enterprise Cloud.

There are also third-party tools to boost your capabilities for secret detection that scan your code to detect hidden passwords, certificated, API or encryption keys to discover vulnerabilities early and remediate immediately. Let’s just mention GitGuardian secret scanning.

Tip # 2 – Restrict and control granted access

One of the main reasons for data breaches as we have already mentioned is the leak of credentials. Now let’s look at this from another angle. The main question here is who granted access to your sensitive data?

For example, you have some temporary developer who joined your DevOps team just for a short period of time and then, after his job has been done and he left the company, your security teams have forgotten to restrict his access. Is that possible? Sure… human mistakes are among the most popular results of data loss.

That’s why proper protection of the password details and the control over whom the access is granted should be always monitored.

Remember about the least privilege rule that can protect you from malicious access and errors.

Tip # 3 – Use GitHub teams

Should everybody of your DevOps team have the same access to every repository? Definitely not. Thus, it is vital to create teams for your organization’s workflows. You can create teams of developers, security engineers, and many others within your organization’s needs and requirements. Moreover, you can grant each of those teams with proper access, e.g. admin, write, or only read.

Tip # 4 – Use 2FA or MFA

Even if you have a strong and reliable password – as you think, it can be compromised. You may use over 16 random characters of lower and upper case, numbers, and signs, but it still can be broken. Is it possible to strengthen the password? Yeap… use 2-factor authentication.

This way of protection serves as an extra layer of security protection during your login process. In addition to the usual information that you provide to log in to your account – username and password, you need to provide some extra piece of data which is known only to you, or you can prove your identity using a device of your personal use. For example, you get a message with a 2FA code or a call on your cell phone.

By the way, GitHub strongly urges you to enable two-factor authentication and provides you with a 2FA recovery code in case you lose access to the telephone. Moreover, GitHub will require all users to enable 2FA by the end of 2023.

On the other hand, for more vital information and N-times stronger security you can enable multiple-factor authentication. In this case, you add more than one piece of extra information and/or device that is known and used only by you.

Tip # 5 – Rotate personal access tokens and SSH keys

You may prefer to use personal access tokens or SSH keys and give up using “common” passwords for authentication to your GitHub account.

Though, don’t forget that it’s worth refreshing them regularly. Another good idea is to protect it with a passphrase or generate your SSH keys on a hardware security key. This will secure and mitigate the potential damage that can lead to your data being leaked or stolen. As if a threat actor gains access to your SSH key, he will be able to push a malicious code to your repo.

Tip # 6 – Ensure code branch protection

GitHub as a service provider has its own set of rules for managing branch protection. It permits users to create such rules in a repository for a single branch, when we speak about some specifications, or all branches in a repository. Though, it’s worth paying attention that if we speak about branches that you want to specify, you shouldn’t forget to use fnmatch syntax.

You may ask why do I need a branch protection as a git security measure? Yet the answer is simple – it reduces the risk of internal threats from one side, and helps avoid pushing the unwanted code into production from the other.

To be more precise let’s look at measures and security features GitHub recommends to take:

  • allow force pushes and deletions
  • restrict the access to those who can push to matching branches
  • lock branch
  • check the required pull request review, status checks and conversation resolution before merging
  • check required signed commits
  • require linear history and merge queue
  • require deployments to succeed before merging

Tip # 7 – Don’t forget to create a SECURITY.md file

Why is it worth including a SECURITY.md file to your development process? Like README.md file, SECURITY.md file contains information about your project. While the first one gives only the general overview of the project, the second one represents the set of instructions which should be followed during a report about security vulnerabilities in your project. Thus, it should include:

  • Disclosure policy, which defines the procedure of how the users should communicate about the discovered information and who they should be in contact with on that issue.
  • Security Update policy, which defines the process of communication of the found vulnerabilities to others.
  • Security-related configuration, which includes the settings that help strengthen the security posture during the project deployment.
  • Future enhancement and known security gaps, which includes some security improvements that your organization hasn’t implemented yet.

Tip # 8 – Disable forking

From the development side, git forking may seem a convenient option for your DevOps team, as it permits you to make a copy of the project and make changes to it without affecting the main branch. Though, from the security side, it has its downsides. First, forking slows down the process of security tracking and makes it a little bit more complicated.

Then, forking gives the possibility to fork all code repos to every member of your DevOps team. What if it is a temporary developer in your team? What will happen if he forks the build for his personal use? The build of your team on which you were working for a long time and put loads of energy, effort and budget?

Tip # 9 – Think twice which GitHub app to include

It’s not a secret that GitHub apps are very useful when it comes to the development process. It can simplify DevOps building and development process, though are all the applications secure?

It is incredibly important to be attentive when you choose any app to add as a feature to your CI/CD process. What you need to do is to read all the reviews thoroughly on how secure software is, check out its producers, and think twice before granting access to any third-party app. You can check its trustworthiness and if it takes all security measures to keep your data protected. For example, if that third-party solution meets SOC 2 or ISO 27K requirements.

Tip # 10 – Backup your GitHub environment

Regular backups can help to withstand different unpleasant situations, like GitHub outages, or ransomware attacks. Though, to have your data secured and easily recoverable from any point in time, it’s better to use the GitHub backup best practices, including:

  • data to backup – make sure that it includes all your GitHub repositories and metadata
  • storage choice – it’s worth choosing a few backup destinations to store your data
  • unlimited retention – that guarantees that your projects will be stored up to forever
  • 3-2-1 backup rule fulfillment – when you have at least 3 copies of your vital data in 2 different storage instances, one of which is offsite
  • ransomware protection – a set of features, that will empower your DevOps team with confidence that the source code build is well-secured
  • encryption – which permits to encrypt the data in-flight and at rest even with your own encryption key
  • automated backups – which can be implemented into your CI/CD process. You, as a CTO or a Security Leader can assign some members of your team to write scripts and perform backups on a regular basis – though, it can be time-consuming. On the other hand, you can use a third-party software, like GitProtect.io which will take the responsibility of data backup and guarantee data accessibility and recoverability.

Tip # 11 – Track the activities via Audit logs

If the organization has a rather complicated structure and a large number of employees, it is a good idea to use Audit logs to track the activity of all the employees from time to time. Audit logs contain the details on who performed the action, when this action was performed and what action it was.

Tip # 12 – Regularly scan your repos

Regular scans for vulnerabilities and bugs can bring more peace to you and your organization itself as your DevOps team will have an opportunity to detect and solve the problems faster. GitHub has its own code scanning feature that can be used for these purposes or you can implement a third-party software, like above mentioned GitGuardian, to scan, detect and remediate bugs in your development process. It will help developers prevent creating new security troubles and work peacefully.

Tip # 13 – Keep your private repositories safe

If you don’t need to use public repositories in your organization, then it’s worth disabling the creation of one. You can easily do it within your GitHub account. It is important as it prevents any member of your DevOps team from accidentally creating publicly accessible repositories. What is more, it reduces the possibility of human mistakes.

Tip # 14 – Manage IP addresses

It is very important to track your DevOps team’s actions, though it might be difficult. As a security measure, you can restrict access to your company’s private assets. What is the best way to do that? Configuring the list of IP addresses allowed to connect.

Using this option, you can grant access only from the IP address of your office network. If you have a remote team, you can approve each single IP access using CIDR notation. Thus, you will have a guarantee that the addresses out of your IP allow list are blocked to the private resources via API, web, or Git.

Tip # 15 – Check source code before importing it into GitHub

It is nice to have a rule to perform a complete audit of your code before importing it to GitHub. It may seem an unimportant issue – to audit a small project, yet imagine that this project grows every day… How much time will your DevOps team need to perform a full audit, update and then pull it? Weeks or even months. Thus, an audit of smaller parts may seem reasonable.

Takeaway

GitHub is an incredibly useful git code management system, yet you shouldn’t underestimate its security. It is important to follow simple steps to protect your intellectual property and assets, which source code is. You can use the features GitHub provides, though don’t forget that GitHub is a service. And as any other SaaS it operates in accordance with the Shared Responsibility Model. What does it mean? The security of your source code is your own duty under the Shared responsibility Model.

With the plethora of features available on GitHub, make sure that you use all necessary ones that will help you to build a reliable secure space for your DevOps team’s continuous coding.

Items.Title: 识别二维形状以及如何在画布上绘制它们。 | Recognizing 2D shapes and how to draw them on a canvas.
Items.Description:
艾莉森艾莉森

发表于 5 月 18 日

我们都知道数字 2。

什么是维度？

维度是特定方向上距离的度量。
想想一条直线。 您可以从 a（左）点移动到 b（右）点，然后再回到 a（左）点。 因为您只能沿着那条线移动，所以它被称为第一维 (1D)。

那什么是二次元呢？

顾名思义，2D 代表二维。 这意味着除了在第一维 (1D) 中左右移动之外，您现在还可以上下移动。 二维有两种方式：左右（水平）和上下（垂直）。

从左到右的点将标记为宽度，从上到下的点将标记为高度。 三角形、矩形、正方形和圆形是二维形状的示例。

二维形状定义为任何只有两个维度的形状：高度和宽度。

我们如何使用编程来绘制二维形状？

这就是画布概念发挥作用的地方。

什么是画布？

画布是在线图形中使用的数字表面。 画布用于创建从简单形状到复杂动画的任何内容。 我们使用 HTML 提供的<canvas>元素在网络浏览器中绘制图形。

要在画布上绘图，我们必须使用脚本。

使用<canvas>元素并不困难。 但是，您确实需要对 HTML 和 Javascript 有基本的了解。

幸运的是，HTML 的高度和宽度属性允许您更改大小。 最好在 html 标签上设置 width 和 height 属性，而不是使用 CSS。 这是由于 CSS 可能会扭曲表单。

使用<canvas>元素的语法。

我们的 HTML 文件中的<canvas>元素如上图所示。

宽度属性。

  • 一个高度属性。
  • 一个唯一的标识符。

    • 未定义时，宽度和高度属性将设置为默认宽度和高度。 唯一标识符 (id) 帮助我们识别脚本中的画布元素，因为绘图将使用 javascript 完成。

    画布上的所有绘图都必须使用 Javascript 完成。

    我们的 Javascript 或脚本文件的语法如上图所示。

    我们脚本中的第一行代码使用 document.getElementById() 函数来定位 '' 元素。 一旦我们有了元素节点，我们就可以使用它的 getContext() 方法来获取绘图上下文。

    我们指定“2D”是因为我们处理的是二维形状。

    在画布上绘图

    现在我们已经设置了画布环境，我们可以开始在上面绘图了。

    我们应该在画布的哪个区域开始绘画？ 两个-

    画布是一个二维网格。

    默认情况下，画布上的所有绘图都从左上角开始，对应坐标 (0,0)。 第一个数字是 x 轴坐标，第二个数字是 y 轴坐标。

    为了更好地理解网格的工作原理，让我们在画布网格上绘制一个矩形。

    结果

    在上面的示例中，我向画布添加了一个边框（颜色：红色），以区分 HTML 画布和我们将要绘制的 2D 形状。

    矩形有四个数字。 第一个 (50) 代表 x 轴，而第二个（也是 50）代表 y 轴。 这意味着我们在 x 轴上从原点移动了 50 个像素，在 y 轴上从原点移动了 50 个像素。

    恭喜！！ 由于我们现在了解画布网格的工作原理，我们将在下一个教程中了解在画布上绘图的注意事项以及如何绘制更多表单。

    Allison Allison

    Posted on May 18

       

    2D is a compound term made up of two words: (2 and dimensions).
    We all know the number 2.

    What is a dimension?

    A dimension is the measurement of distance in a specific direction.
    Think of a straight line. You can move from point a(left) to point b(right), and then back to point a(left). Because you can only move along that line, it is referred to as a first dimension (1D).

    What then is a 2D?

    2D stands for two dimensions, as the name implies. This means that in addition to moving left and right in the first dimension (1D), you may now move up and down. There are two ways in 2D: left-right (horizontal) and up-down (vertical).

    The point from left to right will be labeled width, and the point from top to bottom will be labeled height. Triangles, rectangles, squares, and circles are examples of 2D shapes.

    A 2-dimensional shape is defined as any shape with only two dimensions: height and width.

    How do we use programming to draw 2D shapes?

    That is where the concept of a canvas comes into play.

    What is a Canvas?

    A canvas is a digital surface used in online graphics. A canvas is used to create anything from simple shapes to complex animations. We use the <canvas> element provided by HTML to draw graphics in the web browser.

    The HTML <canvas> element is used for drawing graphics in a web browser. The <canvas> element provides a drawing surface — a container for graphics. To draw on the canvas, we must use a script.

    Using the <canvas> element is not difficult. However, you do need a basic understanding of HTML and Javascript.

    A 'canvas>' has a default size of 300 x 150 pixels (width x height). Fortunately, the HTML height and width properties allow you to change the sizes. It is preferable to set the width and height attributes on the html tag rather than using CSS. This is due to the possibility of CSS distorting the form.

    The syntax for using the <canvas> element.

    A <canvas> element in our HTML file looks like the diagram above.

    Three key characteristics are required for the '' element.

    • a width attribute.
    • a height attribute.
    • a unique identifier.

    When not defined, the width and height attributes will be set to the default width and height. The unique identifier (id) helps us to identify the canvas element in our script since the drawing will be done with javascript.

    All drawings on the canvas must be done with Javascript.

    The syntax of our Javascript or script file looks like the diagram above.

    The first line of code in our script uses the document.getElementById() function to locate the '' element. Once we have the element node, we can use its getContext() method to get the drawing context.

    getContext() is a built-in HTML object with many drawing methods and properties. The getContext() object takes one parameter: the type of context. We specify "2D" because we are dealing with 2D shapes.

    Drawing on a canvas

    Now that we've set up our canvas environment, we can start drawing on it.

    What area of the canvas should we begin painting on? A grid structure is used in the HTML canvas. A two-

    The HTML canvas has a grid system. The canvas is a two-dimensional grid.

    By default, all drawings on the canvas begin in the upper-left corner, which corresponds to the coordinates (0,0). The first number is the x-axis coordinate, and the second number is the y-axis coordinate.

    The x and y axis are made up of values ranging from 0 to the number you choose for the canvas's height and width.
    To better understand how the grid works, let’s draw a rectangle on the canvas grid.

    Result

    From the example above, I added a border(color: red) to the canvas to distinguish the HTML canvas from the 2D shape we will be drawing.

    The rectangle has four numbers. The first (50) represents the x-axis, while the second (also 50) represents the y-axis. This implies that we moved 50 pixels away from the origin on the x-axis and 50 pixels away from the origin on the y-axis.

    Congratulations!! Since we now understand how the canvas grid works, we'll look at the do's and don'ts of drawing on a canvas as well as how to draw more forms in our next tutorial.

Items.Title: 嗯？ | Dependency Injection in... a React application?? Huh?
Items.Description:
您可能从后端工作中了解依赖注入或 DI，或者您可能从 Angular 中记得它。 但我认为您很可能没有考虑将 DI 入侵到 React 应用程序中。 这很好，因为这个想法很奇怪......

但是很有趣！

你们中的一些人可能还记得我在这里发布的关于 AeroDI 的帖子，我在其中构建了自己的依赖项注入库，以获得乐趣和一些有利的用法。 在我所有的业余项目中，我都顽固地在所有可能的地方使用它来捕捉奇怪的错误并使库变得更好。 所以它比原来的帖子更新了一点！
但无论如何，今天是那些奇怪的设计选择之一将允许我们将其放入 React 应用程序的日子。

但为什么？

好问题！ 我不知道是否有人会有类似的用例，但实际上我遇到过使用相同的 DI 有很大帮助的情况。 让我解释。 当我编写后端代码时，我使用了一种叫做领域驱动开发的东西。 这意味着，除其他外，执行业务逻辑的代码与所有内容分离，与 api 绑定、数据库绑定等分离，代码是独立的，独立的，并通过各种接口与外部世界对话（例如，由 api 和 db 实现）。 我将在以后的文章中为这些东西描述一个很好的文件夹结构！ 这里讨论的代码是运行重力多体模拟的代码。 代码的整个域部分在后端与 AeroDI 自动连接。 我意识到我需要在前端使用相同的代码，只是进行一些小的修改，例如不同的文件加载机制。

所以我决定尝试一下

  • 将代码原样复制到前端应用程序到一个特殊的 lib 文件夹中
  • 设置 AeroDI 为该 lib 文件夹生成反射
  • 在应用程序中初始化 AeroDI，但仅初始化一次 之后 DI 应该可以正常工作并且可以像在后端一样使用！ 疯狂的。 让我们看看如何精确地做到这一点。

为了使全局初始化看起来非常专业并且不像 hack，我决定使用 zustand 存储来提供 DI。

所以我对系统做了如下改动：

  • 安装了aero-di包，因为我用的是yarn，with yarn add aero-di
  • 将此添加到 package.json/scripts：
 "generate-reflection": "aero-di-generate --baseDir src/lib"
  • 由 build 和 dev 命令更改以生成反射：
 "dev": "yarn generate-reflection && vite", "build": "yarn generate-reflection && tsc && vite build",
  • 将 DI 添加到 globalStore（为清楚起见，我在此处删除了其他字段）：
 export interface GlobalStore { di: AeroDI; } export const useGlobalStore = create<GlobalStore>((set) => { const di = new AeroDI(classesReflection); return { di }; });

然后，如果你想在 React 组件中使用 DI：

 const di = useGlobalStore((store) => store.di);

例如：

 const getStrongestGravityContributorAtPoint = di.getByClass( GetStrongestGravityContributorAtPoint );

耶！ 简单有效。

解释为什么它甚至有效。

只是一个非常简短的解释，因为它并没有那么多。
这段代码并不是特别特别的东西，放在一个特殊的地方，需要独特的处理。 不，这段代码只是你应用程序中的一些代码片段，就像你应用程序中的任何其他代码一样，将被编译到 dist react 应用程序，在我的例子中是通过 Vite。 而且因为反射元数据是您代码本身的一部分，它将成为 dist 构建的一部分，不需要其他注意！ 简单的。

要记住的一件事是实时重新加载不会重新生成反射数据，但这可以通过设置 nodemon、监视 lib 文件夹并在更改时运行生成来轻松解决，例如：

 { "verbose": true, "watch": ["lib"], "ignore": ["lib/reflectionData.ts"], "ext": "ts,js,json", "exec": "yarn generate-reflection", "delay": 1000 }

就这样

谢谢阅读。 现在我的应用程序中有了这个，我还用它来实现一个 three.js 渲染器，跳过那个可怕的 three.js 反应纤维插件。 我将在下一篇文章中描述如何做到这一点而不是发疯。

在那之前，祝你编码愉快！

一旦暂停，afl_ext 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，afl_ext 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

取消发布后，afl_ext 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 afl_ext 没有暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 afl_ext 的方法：

降低 afl_ext 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 afl_ext 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

You may know the dependency injection, or DI, from your backend endeavours, or maybe you remember it from Angular. But I think it's more than likely you didn't think about hacking the DI into a React app. And that's fine, because the idea is weird...

But very interesting!

Some of you might remember my post about AeroDI here where I build my own dependency injection library for fun and some advantageous usage. I stubbornly use it everywhere I can, in all of my hobby projects, to catch weird bugs and make the library better. So it got updated a bit since the original post!
But anyway, today is the day one of those weird design choices will allow us to do put it into react app.

But why?

Good question! I don't know if anyone will have a similar use case, but I actually had a situation where using the same DI helped a lot. Let me explain. When I write my backend code, I use something called domain driven development. This means, among other things, that the code that does the business logic is separated from everything, from the api binding, from the database binding, etc, the code is on it's own, standalone, and talking to outer world with various interfaces (implemented by api and db, for example). I will describe a nice folder structure for such things in a future article! The code in question here was the code that runs gravitational multibody simulation. The whole domain part of the code is autowired with AeroDI on the backend. I realized I need the same code on the frontend, with just minor corrections like different file loading mechanics.

So I decided to try something

  • Copy the code as is into frontend app into a special lib folder
  • Setup AeroDI to generate reflection for that lib folder
  • Initialize the AeroDI in the app, but only once After that the DI should work fine and can be used just like on the backend! Insane. Let see how to do that precisely.

Zustand, I choose you!!

To make the global initialization look very professional and less like a hack, I decided to use a zustand store to provide the DI.

So I made the following changes to the system:

  • Installed aero-di package, because I use yarn, with yarn add aero-di
  • Added this to package.json/scripts: 
"generate-reflection": "aero-di-generate --baseDir src/lib"
  • Changed by build and dev commands to generate reflection: 
 "dev": "yarn generate-reflection && vite", "build": "yarn generate-reflection && tsc && vite build",
  • Added DI to the globalStore (I removed other fields here for clarity): 
export interface GlobalStore { di: AeroDI;
} export const useGlobalStore = create<GlobalStore>((set) => { const di = new AeroDI(classesReflection); return { di };
});

And then, if you want to use the DI in a react component:

const di = useGlobalStore((store) => store.di);

And for example:

 const getStrongestGravityContributorAtPoint = di.getByClass( GetStrongestGravityContributorAtPoint );

Yay! Simple and works.

Explaination why it even works.

Just a very short explaination, because there is not really that much to it.
AeroDI generates its support code into a typescript file that you then use to initialize the library. This code is not something super special sitting in a special place and needing unique treatment. No, this code is just some code piece in you app, and just like any other code in your app, will be compiled down to the dist react app, in my case via Vite. And because the reflection metadata was part of your code itself, it will become part of the dist build and no other care is needed! Easy.

One thing to remember is live reloading won't regenerate the reflection data, but this can be easily remedied by setting up a nodemon, monitor the lib folder, and run generate on change, for example like that:

{ "verbose": true, "watch": ["lib"], "ignore": ["lib/reflectionData.ts"], "ext": "ts,js,json", "exec": "yarn generate-reflection", "delay": 1000
}

That's all

Thanks for reading. Now that I have this in my app, I used it to also implement a three.js renderer skipping that horrendous three.js react fiber plugin. And I will describe how to do it and not go insane in the next article.

Until then, happy coding!

Items.Title: 使用 GitHub Actions 为 Ubuntu 上的 React 和 Node.js 应用程序自动化 CI/CD | Use GitHub Actions to Automate Your CI/CD for React and Node.js Applications on Ubuntu
Items.Description:
穆罕默德优素福

发表于 5 月 18 日

持续集成和持续部署 (CI/CD) 已成为现代软件开发的重要方面。 通过自动化构建、测试和部署流程，开发人员可以节省时间、减少错误并交付高质量的应用程序。 在本文中，我们将探讨如何在 Ubuntu 环境中为 React 和 Node.js 应用程序的 CI/CD 设置 GitHub Actions。

先决条件：

在深入设置 GitHub Actions 之前，请确保您具备以下条件：

  • 具有 React 或 Node.js 应用程序存储库的 GitHub 帐户。
  • 对 Git 和 GitHub 工作流有基本的了解。
  • 基于 Ubuntu 的机器或虚拟机，用于运行 CI/CD 管道。

设置环境：

  1. 首先在你的 Ubuntu 机器上创建一个新目录来存储 CI/CD 管道所需的文件。
  2. 如果尚未安装 Node.js 和 npm（节点包管理器），请安装它们。 您可以通过运行以下命令来执行此操作：
 sudo apt update sudo apt install nodejs npm

配置 GitHub 操作：

  1. 导航到您的 GitHub 存储库并单击“操作”选项卡。
  2. 单击“自己设置工作流程”按钮以创建新的工作流程文件。
  3. 为文件选择一个名称，例如 ci-cd.yml，并更新内容如下：
 # This workflow is a Continuous Integration/Continuous Deployment (CI/CD) pipeline for deploying a Node.js backend application # to a remote ubuntu server. # # Here are some comments to make it easier for everyone to use: # # 1. The workflow uses environment variables to set the application name, component name, and release path. # Ensure that these are set correctly according to your application's configuration. # # 2. The workflow runs on a push to the main branch and can be manually triggered with inputs to specify the target environment. # # 3. It's important to note that this workflow uses several secrets that need to be set up # in order for it to work properly. You will need to create the following secrets in your repository settings: # # 3.1. REMOTE_SERVER: the IP address or domain name of the remote server you are deploying to. # 3.2. REMOTE_USER: the username you use to SSH into the remote server (root user is recommended). # 3.3. REMOTE_KEY: the SSH private key you use to authenticate with the remote server. # 3.4. ENV_FILE: the contents of your application's .env file. This should be a single-line string with each # environment variable separated by spaces. # 3.5. NGINX_CONFIG: the contents of your NGINX configuration file. This should be a multi-line string with each line # separated by a newline character. # # Overall, this workflow provides a good starting point for deploying a Node.js backend application to a remote server using GitHub Actions. # However, it should be customized according to your application's specific requirements. name: 'CI/CD' env: APP_NAME: 'node' COMPONENT_NAME: 'backend' RELEASE_PATH: /root/node/backend on: push: branches: - main workflow_dispatch: jobs: deploy: runs-on: ubuntu-latest steps: # The first step checks out the code from the repository. - name: Checkout code uses: actions/checkout@v2 # The second step sets up Node.js with version 16. - name: Setup Node.js uses: actions/setup-node@v2 with: node-version: 16 # The third step transfers the code to the remote server using the Secure Copy (SCP) protocol. # The source directory is set to .,!./uploads, which excludes the uploads directory from being transferred. # Ensure that this is set correctly according to your application's file structure. - name: Transfer files to server uses: appleboy/scp-action@master with: host: ${{ secrets.REMOTE_SERVER }} username: ${{ secrets.REMOTE_USER }} key: ${{ secrets.REMOTE_KEY }} rm: true source: '.,!./uploads' target: ${{ env.RELEASE_PATH }}/build # The fourth step creates shared folders for the uploads directory and the node_modules directory and creates symbolic links # to these folders in the current release directory. This helps to ensure that the uploads and node_modules directories # persist across releases and are not overwritten. - name: Create shared folders uses: appleboy/ssh-action@master with: host: ${{ secrets.REMOTE_SERVER }} username: ${{ secrets.REMOTE_USER }} key: ${{ secrets.REMOTE_KEY }} script: | cd ${{ env.RELEASE_PATH }} mkdir -p shared/uploads mkdir -p shared/node_modules ln -sfn ${{ env.RELEASE_PATH }}/shared/uploads ${{ env.RELEASE_PATH }}/build/uploads ln -sfn ${{ env.RELEASE_PATH }}/shared/node_modules ${{ env.RELEASE_PATH }}/build/node_modules # The fifth step copies configuration files to the remote server, including the .env file and an NGINX configuration file. # The NGINX configuration file is used to proxy requests to the Node.js backend application. # Ensure that this is set correctly according to your application and server's configuration. - name: Copy config files uses: appleboy/ssh-action@master with: host: ${{ secrets.REMOTE_SERVER }} username: ${{ secrets.REMOTE_USER }} key: ${{ secrets.REMOTE_KEY }} script: | echo '${{ secrets.ENV_FILE }}' > ${{ env.RELEASE_PATH }}/build/.env echo '${{ vars.NGINX_CONFIG }}' > ${{ env.RELEASE_PATH }}/build/nginx.conf sudo systemctl restart nginx # The sixth step installs dependencies, builds the application. - name: Install dependencies uses: appleboy/ssh-action@master with: host: ${{ secrets.REMOTE_SERVER }} username: ${{ secrets.REMOTE_USER }} key: ${{ secrets.REMOTE_KEY }} script: | cd ${{ env.RELEASE_PATH }}/build yarn install yarn run build # The seventh step starts it with PM2. # PM2 is a process manager for Node.js applications that helps to ensure that the application runs continuously # and can be easily managed. Ensure that this is set correctly according to your application's dependencies and build process. - name: Start PM2 uses: appleboy/ssh-action@master with: host: ${{ secrets.REMOTE_SERVER }} username: ${{ secrets.REMOTE_USER }} key: ${{ secrets.REMOTE_KEY }} script: | cd ${{ env.RELEASE_PATH }}/build pm2 delete -s ${{ env.APP_NAME }} || : pm2 start dist/index.js --name "${{ env.APP_NAME }}"
  1. 在上面的配置中，我们为 Node.js/Express 设置了示例工作流，以在推送到main分支时触发。
  2. 您可以在将应用程序部署到 Heroku、Netlify 或 AWS 等托管服务提供商的步骤中添加/修改部署命令。

运行 CI/CD 管道：

  1. 提交ci-cd.yml文件并将其推送到您的 GitHub 存储库。
  2. 您可以在存储库的“操作”选项卡中监控管道的进度。
  3. 如果在构建或测试阶段出现任何错误，GitHub Actions 将提供详细的日志以帮助您识别和解决问题。

结论：

通过利用工作流的强大功能，您可以节省时间和精力，同时保持代码库的质量。 在本文中，我们探讨了如何在 Ubuntu 环境中使用 GitHub Actions 设置基本的 CI/CD 工作流程。 随意自定义工作流以满足您的特定需求，并利用 GitHub Actions 的丰富生态系统进一步增强您的开发过程。

快乐的自动化！

伊丽莎白莱昂哈特 - 4 月 24 日

原文:

Muhammad Yousuf

Posted on May 18

   

Continuous Integration and Continuous Deployment (CI/CD) have become crucial aspects of modern software development. By automating the build, testing, and deployment processes, developers can save time, reduce errors, and deliver high-quality applications. GitHub Actions, a powerful feature provided by GitHub, allows developers to automate these processes easily. In this article, we will explore how to set up GitHub Actions for CI/CD of React and Node.js applications on an Ubuntu environment.

Prerequisites:

Before diving into setting up GitHub Actions, ensure that you have the following:

  • A GitHub account with a repository for your React or Node.js application.
  • A basic understanding of Git and GitHub workflows.
  • An Ubuntu-based machine or a virtual machine to run the CI/CD pipeline.

Setting Up the Environment:

  1. Start by creating a new directory on your Ubuntu machine to store the necessary files for the CI/CD pipeline.
  2. Install Node.js and npm (Node Package Manager) if they are not already installed. You can do this by running the following command: 
sudo apt update
sudo apt install nodejs npm

Configuring GitHub Actions:

  1. Navigate to your GitHub repository and click on the "Actions" tab.
  2. Click on the "Set up a workflow yourself" button to create a new workflow file.
  3. Choose a name for the file, such as ci-cd.yml, and update the contents as follows: 
# This workflow is a Continuous Integration/Continuous Deployment (CI/CD) pipeline for deploying a Node.js backend application
# to a remote ubuntu server.
#
# Here are some comments to make it easier for everyone to use:
#
# 1. The workflow uses environment variables to set the application name, component name, and release path.
# Ensure that these are set correctly according to your application's configuration.
#
# 2. The workflow runs on a push to the main branch and can be manually triggered with inputs to specify the target environment.
#
# 3. It's important to note that this workflow uses several secrets that need to be set up
# in order for it to work properly. You will need to create the following secrets in your repository settings:
#
# 3.1. REMOTE_SERVER: the IP address or domain name of the remote server you are deploying to.
# 3.2. REMOTE_USER: the username you use to SSH into the remote server (root user is recommended).
# 3.3. REMOTE_KEY: the SSH private key you use to authenticate with the remote server.
# 3.4. ENV_FILE: the contents of your application's .env file. This should be a single-line string with each
# environment variable separated by spaces.
# 3.5. NGINX_CONFIG: the contents of your NGINX configuration file. This should be a multi-line string with each line
# separated by a newline character.
#
# Overall, this workflow provides a good starting point for deploying a Node.js backend application to a remote server using GitHub Actions.
# However, it should be customized according to your application's specific requirements. name: 'CI/CD' env: APP_NAME: 'node' COMPONENT_NAME: 'backend' RELEASE_PATH: /root/node/backend on: push: branches: - main workflow_dispatch: jobs: deploy: runs-on: ubuntu-latest steps: # The first step checks out the code from the repository. - name: Checkout code uses: actions/checkout@v2 # The second step sets up Node.js with version 16. - name: Setup Node.js uses: actions/setup-node@v2 with: node-version: 16 # The third step transfers the code to the remote server using the Secure Copy (SCP) protocol. # The source directory is set to .,!./uploads, which excludes the uploads directory from being transferred. # Ensure that this is set correctly according to your application's file structure. - name: Transfer files to server uses: appleboy/scp-action@master with: host: ${{ secrets.REMOTE_SERVER }} username: ${{ secrets.REMOTE_USER }} key: ${{ secrets.REMOTE_KEY }} rm: true source: '.,!./uploads' target: ${{ env.RELEASE_PATH }}/build # The fourth step creates shared folders for the uploads directory and the node_modules directory and creates symbolic links # to these folders in the current release directory. This helps to ensure that the uploads and node_modules directories # persist across releases and are not overwritten. - name: Create shared folders uses: appleboy/ssh-action@master with: host: ${{ secrets.REMOTE_SERVER }} username: ${{ secrets.REMOTE_USER }} key: ${{ secrets.REMOTE_KEY }} script: | cd ${{ env.RELEASE_PATH }} mkdir -p shared/uploads mkdir -p shared/node_modules ln -sfn ${{ env.RELEASE_PATH }}/shared/uploads ${{ env.RELEASE_PATH }}/build/uploads ln -sfn ${{ env.RELEASE_PATH }}/shared/node_modules ${{ env.RELEASE_PATH }}/build/node_modules # The fifth step copies configuration files to the remote server, including the .env file and an NGINX configuration file. # The NGINX configuration file is used to proxy requests to the Node.js backend application. # Ensure that this is set correctly according to your application and server's configuration. - name: Copy config files uses: appleboy/ssh-action@master with: host: ${{ secrets.REMOTE_SERVER }} username: ${{ secrets.REMOTE_USER }} key: ${{ secrets.REMOTE_KEY }} script: | echo '${{ secrets.ENV_FILE }}' > ${{ env.RELEASE_PATH }}/build/.env echo '${{ vars.NGINX_CONFIG }}' > ${{ env.RELEASE_PATH }}/build/nginx.conf sudo systemctl restart nginx # The sixth step installs dependencies, builds the application. - name: Install dependencies uses: appleboy/ssh-action@master with: host: ${{ secrets.REMOTE_SERVER }} username: ${{ secrets.REMOTE_USER }} key: ${{ secrets.REMOTE_KEY }} script: | cd ${{ env.RELEASE_PATH }}/build yarn install yarn run build # The seventh step starts it with PM2. # PM2 is a process manager for Node.js applications that helps to ensure that the application runs continuously # and can be easily managed. Ensure that this is set correctly according to your application's dependencies and build process. - name: Start PM2 uses: appleboy/ssh-action@master with: host: ${{ secrets.REMOTE_SERVER }} username: ${{ secrets.REMOTE_USER }} key: ${{ secrets.REMOTE_KEY }} script: | cd ${{ env.RELEASE_PATH }}/build pm2 delete -s ${{ env.APP_NAME }} || : pm2 start dist/index.js --name "${{ env.APP_NAME }}"
  1. In the above configuration, we set up the sample workflow for Node.js/Express to trigger on pushes to the main branch.
  2. The build job checks out the repository, sets up Node.js version 16, transfer files to the server, installs dependencies, creates the build and restart PM2 service.
  3. You can add/modify deployment commands in the steps to deploy your application to a hosting provider like Heroku, Netlify, or AWS.

Running the CI/CD Pipeline:

  1. Commit and push the ci-cd.yml file to your GitHub repository.
  2. GitHub Actions will automatically detect the new workflow and start the CI/CD pipeline.
  3. You can monitor the progress of the pipeline in the "Actions" tab of your repository.
  4. If any errors occur during the build or test stages, GitHub Actions will provide detailed logs to help you identify and resolve issues.

Conclusion:

GitHub Actions offers a convenient way to automate your CI/CD pipeline for React and Node.js applications. By leveraging the power of workflows, you can save time and effort while maintaining the quality of your codebase. In this article, we explored how to set up a basic CI/CD workflow using GitHub Actions on an Ubuntu environment. Feel free to customize the workflow to suit your specific needs and take advantage of the rich ecosystem of GitHub Actions to enhance your development process further.

Happy automating!

Elisabeth Leonhardt - Apr 24

Items.Title: 为什么安全性在云技术中如此重要？ | Why security is so important in cloud technologies?
Items.Description:
梅苏特厄兹迪尔

发表于 5 月 18 日

为什么安全性在云技术中如此重要？
云技术提供了通过互联网存储和管理数据和应用程序的可能性。 因此，安全措施至关重要。 以下是云安全很重要的几个原因：

如果没有适当的安全措施，数据可能容易受到未经授权的访问。

安全漏洞可能导致未经授权查看或窃取数据。

数据不应被篡改或损坏，因为它会影响信息的可靠性和准确性。

安全漏洞可能导致攻击、数据丢失或服务中断。

Why security is so important in cloud technologies?
🌐🔒

🤔

Cloud technologies offer the possibility to store and manage data and applications over the internet. Therefore, security measures are critical. 🔍

Here are a few reasons why cloud security is important:

1️⃣ Data Protection: 😱🔒 Cloud technology deals with storing and processing sensitive data. Without proper security measures, data can be vulnerable to unauthorized access.

2️⃣ Privacy: 🤐🔐 Cloud service providers are responsible for safeguarding the privacy of user data. Security vulnerabilities can lead to unauthorized viewing or theft of data.

3️⃣ Data Integrity: 🔄🔐 Maintaining the integrity of data stored in the cloud is crucial. Data should not be tampered with or corrupted, as it affects the reliability and accuracy of information.

4️⃣ Service Continuity: ⏰🔒 Cloud service providers must ensure uninterrupted access to applications and data for users. Security loopholes can result in attacks, data loss, or service disruptions.

5️⃣ Compliance: 📜🔒 Many industries have specific security and compliance standards for data storage and processing. Cloud services need to meet these requirements and ensure compliance.

Cloud security is essential to safeguard user data, privacy, integrity, service continuity, and compliance. Neglecting security measures can expose users to significant risks and potential attacks. 🚫🔓

Items.Title: 将 Cloudinary-Storage 与 Django 集成 🔗🔥 | Integrating Cloudinary-Storage with Django 🔗🔥
Items.Description:
舒巴吉特普拉丹

发表于 5 月 18 日

在本文中，我将向您解释如何将 Cloudinary-Storage 与 Django 集成。 这将允许您使用 Cloudinary 的功能来管理 Django 应用程序中的图像和视频。

先决条件
在开始之前，您需要具备以下条件：

  • 您可以在以下位置注册一个免费帐户

一个 Django 项目。 您可以使用以下命令创建一个新的 Django 项目：

 Django-admin startproject project_name

为 Django 安装 Cloudinary-Storage Cloudinary-Storage 是一个 Django 包，它提供了一个用于与 Cloudinary 交互的 Python API。 运行以下命令安装 Cloudinary 包：

pip install Django-Cloudinary-storage

在提及您的api keys and storage keys之前，不要忘记在您的INSTALLED APSS中提及cloudinary_storage 。 它看起来像这样：

 # Application definition INSTALLED_APPS = [ 'django.contrib.admin', 'django.contrib.auth', 'django.contrib.contenttypes', 'django.contrib.sessions', 'django.contrib.messages', 'django.contrib.staticfiles', #important 'project_name', 'base.apps.BaseConfig', 'crispy_forms', #optional 'django_filters', 'ckeditor', #optional 'ckeditor_uploader', #optional 'storages', 'cloudinary_storage', #important ]

在 Django 中配置 Cloudinary
要在 Django 中配置 Cloudinary，您需要将以下设置添加到 Django 项目的settings.py文件中：

 CLOUDINARY_STORAGE = { 'CLOUD_NAME': 'your_cloud_name', 'API_KEY': 'CLOUDINARY_API', 'API_SECRET': 'CLOUDINARY_API_SECRET' }

注意：在发布/部署您的项目之前，请确保您隐藏了您的 api 秘密和密钥。

使用环境变量为了保证 api 密钥的安全，应该始终使用环境变量。 像这样存储您的 API 密钥或任何秘密密钥：

 CLOUDINARY_STORAGE = { 'CLOUD_NAME': 'your_cloud_name', 'API_KEY': os.environ.get('CLOUDINARY_API'), 'API_SECRET': os.environ.get('CLOUDINARY_API_SECRET') }

上传图片到 Cloudinary
在 Django 中配置 Cloudinary 后，您可以将图像上传到 Cloudinary，但首先，您必须做最后一件事，即按照以下方式在settings.py中配置文件存储：

DEFAULT_FILE_STORAGE = 'cloudinary_storage.storage.MediaCloudinaryStorage'

这将允许您将图像存储在 Cloudinary 云存储中，如果您想上传任何文本文件/视频，则可以使用以下方法：

 cloudinary_storage.storage.VideoMediaCloudinaryStorage #for videos cloudinary_storage.storage.RawMediaCloudinaryStorage # for text files

结语📌
在本文中，我向您展示了如何将 Cloudinary-Storage 与 Django 集成。 这将允许您使用 Cloudinary 的功能来管理您的网络应用程序中的媒体文件。 您可以在django-cloudinary-storage 0.3.0上找到 Cloudinary-Storage 的其他功能，以获得更令人兴奋的定制。
如果您觉得它有用，请分享并表示支持🙏🏼这篇文章💙。 写博客快乐😊

原文:

Shubhajeet Pradhan

Posted on May 18

Cloudinary is a cloud-based photo and video managing service that offers picture and video uploading, storage, distribution, and transformation among other services. Django is a well-known Python web-based framework that can be used to create a wide range of online applications.

In this article, I'll explain to you how you can integrate Cloudinary-Storage with Django. This will allow you to use Cloudinary' s features to manage your images and videos in your Django applications.

Prerequisites
Before you begin, you will need the following:

  • A Cloudinary account. You can sign up for a free account at

cloudinary.com/

  • A Django project. You can create a new Django project using the following command: 
Django-admin startproject project_name

Installing the Cloudinary-Storage for Django The Cloudinary-Storage is a Django package which provides a Python API for interacting with Cloudinary. Run the below command to install the package of Cloudinary:

pip install Django-Cloudinary-storage

After successful installation of Cloudinary-Storage, you need to import the following in your settings.py file

import cloudinary
import cloudinary.uploader
import cloudinary.api
import cloudinary_storage

Before mentioning your api keys and storage keys, don't forget to mention cloudinary_storage in your INSTALLED APSS. It would appear something like this:

# Application definition

INSTALLED_APPS = [
    'django.contrib.admin',
    'django.contrib.auth',
    'django.contrib.contenttypes',
    'django.contrib.sessions',
    'django.contrib.messages',
    'django.contrib.staticfiles', #important
    'project_name',
    'base.apps.BaseConfig',
    'crispy_forms', #optional
    'django_filters',
    'ckeditor', #optional
    'ckeditor_uploader', #optional
    'storages',
    'cloudinary_storage', #important
]

Configuring Cloudinary in Django
To configure Cloudinary in Django, you will need to add the following settings to your Django project's settings.py file:

CLOUDINARY_STORAGE = {
    'CLOUD_NAME': 'your_cloud_name',
    'API_KEY': 'CLOUDINARY_API',
    'API_SECRET': 'CLOUDINARY_API_SECRET'
}

NOTE: Before publishing/deploying your project, make sure you hide your api secret & keys.

Using Environment Variable For keeping api secret keys safe, one should always use environment variables. Store your API keys or any secret keys like this:

CLOUDINARY_STORAGE = {
    'CLOUD_NAME': 'your_cloud_name',
    'API_KEY': os.environ.get('CLOUDINARY_API'),
    'API_SECRET': os.environ.get('CLOUDINARY_API_SECRET')
}

Uploading Images to Cloudinary
Once you have configured Cloudinary in Django, you can upload images to Cloudinary but first, you have to do one last thing and that is to configure your file storage in settings.py in the below-mentioned way:

DEFAULT_FILE_STORAGE = 'cloudinary_storage.storage.MediaCloudinaryStorage'

This will allow you to store images in your Cloudinary cloud storage and if you want to upload any text files/ videos then you can use the following:

cloudinary_storage.storage.VideoMediaCloudinaryStorage #for videos
cloudinary_storage.storage.RawMediaCloudinaryStorage # for text files

Conclusion📌
In this article, I've shown you how to integrate Cloudinary-Storage with Django. This will allow you to use Cloudinary' s features to manage your media files in your web apps. There are other features of Cloudinary-Storage which you can find on django-cloudinary-storage 0.3.0 for more exciting customization.
If you find it useful then please share and show some support 🙏🏼 this post 💙. Happy Blogging 😊

Items.Title: IT 公司的外包人力资源：选择合适的供应商时要考虑的 5 个关键因素 | Outsourcing HR for IT Companies: 5 Key Factors to Consider When Choosing the Right Provider
Items.Description:
对于希望专注于核心业务而将 HR 职能留给专家的 IT 公司而言，外包 HR 可能是一种明智的策略。 然而，找到合适的人力资源外包供应商对于确保成功的合作伙伴关系至关重要。 在本文中，我们将探讨 IT 公司在外包 HR 时应考虑的五个关键因素。

经验和专业知识

在外包人力资源方面，经验和专业知识很重要。 供应商应深入了解 IT 公司面临的独特人力资源挑战，例如吸引和留住顶尖人才，以及遵守多个司法管辖区的法规。 他们还应该拥有与 IT 公司成功合作的良好记录。

文化契合度

外包人力资源时，文化是一个重要的考虑因素。 提供商应了解 IT 公司运营所在国家/地区的文化差异，以及 IT 公司本身的独特文化。 通过寻找具有相似文化的供应商，IT 公司可以确保他们的人力资源实践符合他们的整体公司文化。

沟通技巧

沟通是任何业务关系的关键，外包人力资源时尤其如此。 提供者应提供及时和透明的更新，并可以回答问题并根据需要提供指导。

技术能力

在当今的数字时代，人力资源外包提供商应具备强大的技术能力，以帮助 IT 公司高效、有效地管理其人力资源流程。 提供商应提供先进的 HR 软件解决方案，以帮助 IT 公司简化其 HR 流程并改善结果。 他们还应具备数据隐私和安全方面的专业知识，以确保敏感的员工信息得到保护。

成本效益

外包人力资源时，成本效益是一个重要的考虑因素。 虽然成本不应该是决策过程中的唯一因素，但重要的是要找到一家提供有竞争力的价格而又不牺牲质量或专业知识的供应商。 提供商应该对其定价结构保持透明，并清楚地了解其费用中包含的服务。

雇用 StepUp 的好处

他们为在人力资源职能、招聘和员工扩充方面需要帮助的美国 IT 公司提供全面的解决方案。

通过与 StepUp 合作，IT 公司可以受益于：

  • 超过10年的IT外包行业经验
  • 在人才招聘、员工保留和合规管理方面的深厚专业知识
  • 双语招聘人员团队可以与拉丁美洲的候选人以及美国的 IT 公司进行有效沟通

结论

外包人力资源可以帮助 IT 公司改善他们的人力资源流程和成果，但找到合适的供应商至关重要。 通过考虑经验、文化契合度、沟通技巧、技术能力和成本效益等因素，IT 公司可以在选择像Step-Up这样的人力资源外包提供商时做出明智的决定。

Outsourcing HR can be a smart strategy for IT companies that want to focus on their core business while leaving HR functions to experts. However, finding the right HR outsourcing provider is crucial to ensure a successful partnership. In this article, we’ll explore five key factors that IT companies should consider when outsourcing HR.

Experience and Expertise

When it comes to outsourcing HR, experience and expertise matter. IT companies should look for an HR outsourcing provider that has a track record of success in the technology industry. The provider should have a deep understanding of the unique HR challenges faced by IT companies, such as attracting and retaining top talent, and complying with regulations in multiple jurisdictions. They should also have a proven track record of successful partnerships with IT companies.

Cultural Fit

Culture is an important consideration when outsourcing HR. IT companies should seek a provider that has a cultural fit with their own company. The provider should understand the cultural nuances of the countries where the IT company operates, as well as the unique culture of the IT company itself. By finding a provider with a similar culture, IT companies can ensure that their HR practices align with their overall company culture.

Communication Skills

Communication is key in any business relationship, and this is especially true when it comes to outsourcing HR. IT companies should look for a provider that has strong communication skills and can adapt to their preferred communication channels. The provider should provide timely and transparent updates, and be available to answer questions and provide guidance as needed.

Technology Capabilities

In today’s digital age, HR outsourcing providers should have strong technical capabilities to help IT companies manage their HR processes efficiently and effectively. The provider should offer advanced HR software solutions that can help IT companies streamline their HR processes and improve outcomes. They should also have expertise in data privacy and security, to ensure that sensitive employee information is protected.

Cost-Effectiveness

Cost-effectiveness is an important consideration when outsourcing HR. While cost shouldn’t be the only factor in the decision-making process, it’s important to find a provider that offers competitive pricing without sacrificing quality or expertise. The provider should be transparent about their pricing structure and offer a clear understanding of the services included in their fees.

The Benefits of Hiring StepUp

StepUp is an HR outsourcing provider that specializes in helping IT companies recruit and augment staff from Latin America. They offer a comprehensive solution for US-based IT companies that need help with HR functions, recruitment, and staff augmentation.

By partnering with StepUp, IT companies can benefit from:

  • Over 10 years of experience in the IT outsourcing industry
  • Deep expertise in talent acquisition, employee retention, and compliance management
  • A bilingual team of recruiters who can communicate effectively with candidates in Latin America, as well as with IT companies in the US
  • Advanced HR software solutions, such as applicant tracking systems, performance management tools, and employee self-service portals
  • Competitive pricing that reduces overhead costs for IT companies
  • A large talent pool of IT professionals from Latin America that can be quickly and efficiently vetted and hired for specific needs
  • Staff augmentation services that can help IT companies scale up their teams quickly and efficiently

Conclusion

Outsourcing HR can help IT companies improve their HR processes and outcomes, but finding the right provider is crucial. By considering factors like experience, cultural fit, communication skills, technology capabilities, and cost-effectiveness, IT companies can make an informed decision when choosing an HR outsourcing provider like Step-Up.

Items.Title: React 协调：它是如何工作的以及我们为什么要关心 | React reconciliation: how it works and why should we care
Items.Description:
表中的内容

本文以视频格式提供。 细节较少，但动画和声音不错，而不是字母😉

每次我认为我肯定知道关于 React 如何渲染组件的一切时，宇宙总能找到让我惊讶的方法。 像简单的if语句这样天真无邪的东西会让你大吃一惊。 根据我墙上的“周末待办事项”列表，就在这个星期六，当我随机浏览 React 文档作为拖延我实际应该做的事情时再次发生。 又一次“等一下，这不对”导致又一个周末计划化为泡影，随后又进行了另一次深入调查和文章。 无论如何，谁需要那些 ToDo 事情？ 他们并不重要，对吧？

因此，让我们来看看破坏我周末承诺的那个小谜团，了解协调算法的工作原理，并回答其他几个问题。 你有没有想过为什么如果我们在其他组件中创建组件，它们会在每次重新渲染时重新安装自己？ 或者为什么我们不需要列表之外的“键”属性，即使我们以相同的行为呈现相同的数据？ 是时候找出答案了！

首先，谜团本身。

假设您有条件地渲染一个组件。 如果是“某物”- 给我看这个组件。 否则，给我看别的东西。 例如，我正在为我的网站开发一个“注册”表格，该表格的一部分是注册的人是公司还是普通人。 出于一些疯狂的税收目的。 所以我只想在用户单击“是的，我正在注册公司”复选框后才显示“公司税号”输入字段。 对于人们，显示文本“你不必向我们提供你的税号，幸运的人。”

 const Form = () => { const [isCompany, setIsCompany] = useState(false); return ( <> ... // checkbox somewhere here {isCompany ? ( <Input id="company-tax-id-number" placeholder="Enter you company ID" ... /> ) : ( <TextPlaceholder /> )} </> ) }

如果用户实际上声称他们是一家公司并且值isCompany从默认的false更改为true ，那么从重新呈现和安装的角度来看，这里会发生什么？

毫不奇怪，答案非常直观： Form组件将重新呈现自身， TextPlaceholder组件将被卸载， Input组件将被安装。 如果我向后翻转复选框，将再次安装Input ，并卸载TextPlaceholder 。

从行为的角度来看，所有这一切都意味着如果我在Input中输入一些东西，勾选复选框，然后将其翻转回来——我在那里输入的任何内容都会丢失。 但是，如果我实际上也需要向人们收集税号，会发生什么情况？ 该字段的外观和行为应该完全相同，但它会有不同的id 、不同的onChange回调和其他不同的设置。 自然地，我会做这样的事情：

 const Form = () => { const [isCompany, setIsCompany] = useState(false); return ( <> ... // checkbox somewhere here {isCompany ? ( <Input id="company-tax-id-number" placeholder="Enter you company Tax ID" ... /> ) : ( <Input id="person-tax-id-number" placeholder="Enter you personal Tax ID" ... /> )} </> ) }

现在这里会发生什么？

当然，答案又是非常直观的，并且与任何明智的人所期望的完全一样。 卸载不再发生！ 如果我在字段中输入内容然后翻转复选框，文本仍然存在！ 这是使用它的代码沙箱。 打开控制台，看看当您在第一个示例中来回翻转复选框但在第二个示例中从未发生时，安装是如何发生的。

如果你对此一点都不感到惊讶并且可以毫不犹豫地说：“啊，是的，这是因为[原因]”那么哇，我可以得到你的签名吗？ 对于我们其他人来说，因为这种行为而眼睛抽搐和轻微头痛，是时候深入研究 React 的协调过程来获得答案了。

这都是因为DOM以及向其添加或删除任何内容的速度有多慢。 当我们在 React 中编写任何内容时，我们的最终目标是让 React 将我们提供给它的任何内容转换为具有适当数据的屏幕上的 DOM 元素。 当我们这样写代码时：

 const Input = ({ placeholder }) => { return <input type="text" id={id} /> } // somewhere else <Input placeholder="Input something here" />

我们希望 React 在 DOM 结构的适当位置添加带有placeholder设置的普通 HTML input标签。 如果我们更改 React 组件中的placeholder值，我们希望 React 使用新值更新我们的 DOM 元素并在屏幕上看到该值。 理想情况下立即。 所以我们不能只删除以前的输入，然后用新数据附加一个新的输入。 那会非常慢。 相反，我们需要识别该输入的 DOM 元素并仅更新其属性。 如果我们没有 React，我们将不得不做这样的事情：

 const input = document.getElementById('input-id'); input.placeholder = "new data";

幸运的是，我们不再需要手动执行此操作。 它通过创建和修改我们有时称为“虚拟 DOM”的东西来实现。 这个 Virtual DOM 只是一个巨大的对象，包含所有应该渲染的组件、它们的所有道具和它们的子对象——它们也是具有相同形状的对象。 只是一棵树。 上面示例中的Input组件应该呈现的内容将表示为如下所示：

 { type: "input", // type of element that we need to render props: {...}, // input's props like id or placeholder ... // bunch of other internal stuff }

如果我们的Input组件正在渲染更多内容：

 const Input = () => { return <> <label htmlFor={id}>{label}</label> <input type="text" id={id} /> </> }

那么来自 React 透视图的label和input将只是这些对象的数组：

 [ { type: 'label', ... // other stuff }, { type: 'input', ... // other stuff } ]

但是如果我们渲染 React 组件，它们并不直接与 DOM 元素相关，所以 React 需要以某种方式解决这个问题。

 const Component = () => { return <Input /> }

在这种情况下，它将把组件的功能作为“类型”。 它只是获取我们称为Input组件的整个功能并将其放在那里：

 { type: Input, // reference to that Input function we declared earlier ... // other stuff }

然后，当 React 收到安装应用程序的命令（初始渲染）时，它会遍历该树并执行以下操作：

  • 如果“类型”是一个字符串，它会生成该类型的 HTML 元素。
  • 如果“类型”是一个函数（即我们的组件），它会调用它并遍历该函数返回的树。

直到它最终获得准备显示的整个 DOM 节点树。 像这样的组件，例如：

 const Component = () => { return ( <div> <Input placeholder="Text1" id="1" /> <Input placeholder="Text2" id="2" /> </div> ) }

将表示为：

 { type: 'div', props: { // children are props! children: [ { type: Input, props: { id: "1", placeholder: "Text1" } }, { type: Input, props: { id: "2", placeholder: "Text2" } } ] } }

这将在安装时解析为 HTML，如下所示：

 <div> <input placeholder="Text1" id="1" /> <input placeholder="Text2" id="2" /> </div>

最后，当一切准备就绪时，React 使用 JavaScript 的appendChild命令将这些 DOM 元素附加到实际document中。

之后，乐趣就开始了。 假设该树中的一个组件具有状态，并且触发了它的更新（触发了重新渲染）。 或者可能添加或删除一些新元素。

因此，它再次开始通过该树的旅程，从启动状态更新的地方开始。 如果我们有这段代码：

 const Component = () => { // return just one element return <Input /> }

它将比较状态更新“之前”和“之后”该对象的“类型”字段。 如果类型相同， Input组件将被标记为“需要更新”，并触发其重新渲染。 如果类型已更改，则 React 在重新渲染周期中将删除（卸载）“前一个”组件并添加（安装）“下一个”组件。 在我们的例子中，“类型”将是相同的，因为它只是对函数的引用，并且该引用没有改变。

如果我用那个Input做一些有条件的事情，比如返回另一个组件：

 const Component = () => { if (isCompany) return <Input /> return <TextPlaceholder /> }

然后，假设更新是由isCompany值从true翻转为false触发的，React 将比较的对象是：

 // Before update, isCompany was "true" { type: Input, ... } // After update, isCompany is "false" { type: TextPlaceholder, ... }

结果你猜对了吗？ 并且会首次挂载新的TextPlaceholder组件并将其附加到 DOM。 与Input字段关联的所有内容，包括其状态和您在此处键入的所有内容，都将被销毁。

现在让我们带着新的知识从头看一下神秘的代码：

 const Form = () => { const [isCompany, setIsCompany] = useState(false); return ( <> ... // checkbox somewhere here {isCompany ? ( <Input id="company-tax-id-number" placeholder="Enter you company Tax ID" ... /> ) : ( <Input id="person-tax-id-number" placeholder="Enter you personal Tax ID" ... /> )} </> ) }

如果这里isCompany变量从true变为false ，会比较哪些对象？

之前， isCompany为true ：

 { type: Input, ... // the rest of the stuff, including props like id="company-tax-id-number" }

之后， isCompany为false ：

 { type: Input, ... // the rest of the stuff, including props like id="person-tax-id-number" }

从 React 的角度来看，“类型”没有改变。 它们都引用了完全相同的功能： Input组件。 因此，在这种情况下，React 会按照教给它的方式进行操作：它只获取现有的Input组件并使用新数据对其进行更新。 即，重新渲染它。 与现有Input关联的所有内容（如其 DOM 元素或状态）仍然存在。 什么都没有被摧毁。 这导致了我们所看到的行为：我在输入中输入了一些内容，勾选了复选框，但文本仍然存在。

这种行为不一定是坏的。 我可以看到重新渲染而不是重新安装的情况正是我想要的。 但在这种情况下，我可能想修复它并确保每次在它们之间切换时重置和重新安装输入：从业务逻辑的角度来看它们是不同的实体，所以我不想重复使用他们。

至少有两种简单的方法可以修复它：数组和键。

到现在为止，我只提到了该数据树中数组的事实。 但是任何人都不可能编写出每个组件只返回一个元素的 React 应用程序。 我们现在必须更详细地讨论元素数组以及它们在重新渲染期间的行为方式。 即使是我们简单的Form实际上也有一个数组：

 const Form = () => { const [isCompany, setIsCompany] = useState(false); return ( <> ... // checkbox somewhere here {isCompany ? ( <Input id="company-tax-id-number" ... /> ) : ( <Input id="person-tax-id-number" ... /> )} </> ) }

它返回一个片段（那个东西： <>...</> ），它有一个子数组：那里隐藏了一个复选框。 实际代码更像这样：

 const Form = () => { const [isCompany, setIsCompany] = useState(false); return ( <> <Checkbox onChange={() => setIsCompany(!isCompany)} /> {isCompany ? ( <Input id="company-tax-id-number" ... /> ) : ( <Input id="person-tax-id-number" ... /> )} </> ) }

在重新渲染期间，当 React 看到子项数组而不是单个项时，它只是对其进行迭代，然后根据它们在数组中的位置比较“之前”和“之后”元素及其“类型”。

基本上，如果我翻转复选框并触发Form重新渲染，React 将看到这个项目数组：

 [ { type: Checkbox, }, { type: Input, // our conditional input } ]

并将一一通过它们。 第一要素。 之前的“类型”： Checkbox ，之后的“类型”：也是Checkbox 。 重新使用它并重新渲染它。 第二要素。 同样的程序。 等等。

即使其中一些元素像这样有条件地呈现：

 isCompany ? <Input /> : null

只是有时，这些项目将为null 。 如果我像这样重写Form ：

 const Form = () => { const [isCompany, setIsCompany] = useState(false); return ( <> <Checkbox onChange={() => setIsCompany(!isCompany)} /> {isCompany ? <Input id="company-tax-id-number" ... /> : null} {!isCompany ? <Input id="person-tax-id-number" ... /> : null} </> ) }

它将是始终包含三个项目的数组： Checkbox 、 Input或null,以及Input或 null。

那么，当状态更改并重新呈现整个表单时，这里会发生什么？

之前， isCompany是false ：

 [ { type: Checkbox }, null, { type: Input } ]

之后， isCompany为true ：

 [ { type: Checkbox }, { type: Input }, null ]

当 React 开始逐项比较它们时，它将是：

  • 第一项， Checkbox前后→重新渲染Checkbox
  • 第二项， null before and Input after → mount Input
  • 第三项，之前Input ，之后为null → unmount Item

并且 💥：神奇的是，通过更改输入在渲染输出中的位置，而不更改逻辑中的任何其他内容，错误得到修复，并且输入的行为完全符合我的预期！ 请参阅代码沙箱中的完整示例

还有另一种修复相同错误的方法：借助“key”属性。

任何在 React 中编写过任何列表的人都应该熟悉“键”。 当我们遍历数据数组时，React 强制我们添加它：

 const data = ["1", "2"]; const Component = () => { // "key" is mandatory here! return data.map(value => <Input key={value} />) }

这个组件的输出现在应该很清楚了：只是一个具有“类型” Input的对象数组：

 [ { type: Input }, // "1" data item { type: Input } // "2" data item ]

但是这样的动态列表的问题在于它们是动态的。 我们可以重新排序它们，在开头或结尾添加新项目，并且通常只是乱搞它们。

现在 React 面临一个有趣的任务：该数组中的所有组件都是同一类型。 如何检测哪个是哪个？ 如果这些项目的顺序发生变化：

 [ { type: Input }, // "2" data item now, but React doesn't know that { type: Input } // "1" data item now, but React doesn't know that ]

如何确保重新使用正确的现有元素？ 因为如果它只依赖于该数组中元素的顺序，它将为第二个元素的数据重新使用第一个元素的实例，反之亦然。 如果这些项目有状态，这将导致奇怪的行为：它将保留在第一个项目中。 如果您在第一个输入字段中键入内容并重新排列数组，则键入的文本将保留在第一个输入中。

这就是我们需要“key”的原因：它基本上是 React 版本的子数组中元素的唯一标识符，在重新渲染之间使用。 如果一个元素有一个与“类型”并行的“键”，那么在重新渲染期间，如果“键”和“类型”匹配“之前”，React 将重新使用现有元素及其所有关联的状态和 DOM “ 之后。” 不管他们在阵中的位置如何。

使用此数组，数据将如下所示。 重新订购前：

 [ { type: Input, key: "1" }, // "1" data item { type: Input, key: "2" } // "2" data item ]

重新订购后：

 [ { type: Input, key: "2" }, // "2" data item, React knows that because of "key" { type: Input, key: "1" } // "1" data item, React knows that because of "key" ]

现在，有了密钥，React 就会知道在重新渲染后，它需要重新使用曾经位于第一个位置的已经创建的元素。 所以它只会交换input DOM 节点。 我们在第一个元素中键入的文本将随之移动到第二个位置。 有关不同键的行为方式的更多示例，本文可能会有用： www.developerway.com/posts/react-key-at ...

请参阅 codesandbox 中的完整示例

为什么所有这些对我们的Form组件及其错误都很重要？ 有趣的事实：“键”只是元素的一个属性，它不仅限于动态数组。 在任何子数组中，它的行为方式完全相同。 正如我们已经发现的那样，我们的Form的对象定义从一开始就存在错误：

 const Form = () => { const [isCompany, setIsCompany] = useState(false); return ( <> <Checkbox onChange={() => setIsCompany(!isCompany)} /> {isCompany ? ( <Input id="company-tax-id-number" ... /> ) : ( <Input id="person-tax-id-number" ... /> )} </> ) }

有一系列孩子：

 [ { type: Checkbox }, { type: Input } // react thinks it's the same input between re-renders ]

我们修复初始错误所需要做的就是让 React 意识到重新渲染之间的那些Input组件实际上是不同的组件，不应该被重复使用。 如果我们为这些输入添加一个“键”，我们就可以实现这一点。

 {isCompany ? ( <Input id="company-tax-id-number" key="company-tax-id-number" ... /> ) : ( <Input id="person-tax-id-number" key="person-tax-id-number" ... /> )}

现在，重新渲染前后的子数组将会改变。

之前， isCompany是false ：

 [ { type: Checkbox }, { type: Input, key: "person-tax-id-number" } ]

之后， isCompany为true ：

 [ { type: Checkbox }, { type: Input, key: "company-tax-id-number" } ]

砰，钥匙不一样了！ 当我们在输入之间切换时，状态现在重置为空。

在codeandbox中查看

另一个有趣的事实：如果我们确实需要重用现有元素，“键”也可以提供帮助。 还记得这段代码吗，我们通过在子数组中的不同位置渲染Input元素来修复错误？

 const Form = () => { const [isCompany, setIsCompany] = useState(false); return ( <> <Checkbox onChange={() => setIsCompany(!isCompany)} /> {isCompany ? <Input id="company-tax-id-number" ... /> : null} {!isCompany ? <Input id="person-tax-id-number" ... /> : null} </> ) }

当isCompany状态变量发生变化时， Input组件将卸载和挂载，因为它们位于数组中的不同位置。 但！ 如果我将“key”属性添加到具有相同值的这两个输入，魔法就会发生。

 <> <Checkbox onChange={() => setIsCompany(!isCompany)} /> {isCompany ? <Input id="company-tax-id-number" key="tax-input" ... /> : null} {!isCompany ? <Input id="person-tax-id-number" key="tax-input" ... /> : null} </>

从数据和重新渲染的角度来看，现在是这样的。

之前， isCompany是false ：

 [ { type: Checkbox }, null, { type: Input, key: "tax-input" } ]

之后， isCompany为true ：

 [ { type: Checkbox }, { type: Input, key: "tax-input" } null ]

所以它会认为Input组件只是改变了它在数组中的位置，并且会为它重新使用已经创建的实例。 如果我们输入一些东西，即使Input在技术上是不同的，状态也会被保留。

对于这个特定的例子，这当然只是一种奇怪的行为，在实践中并不是很有用。 但我可以想象它被用于微调组件的性能，如手风琴、标签内容或一些画廊。

既然谜团已经解开并且算法或多或少地清晰了，那么让我们从和解中获得更多乐趣。 那里仍然有一些小问题和谜团。 例如，您是否注意到 React 从不强制您向任何内容添加键，除非您遍历数组？

这个的对象定义：

 const data = ["1", "2"]; const Component = () => { // "key" is mandatory here! return <>{data.map(value => <Input key={value} />)}</> }

和这个：

 const Component = () => { // no-one cares about "key" here return ( <> <Input /> <Input /> </> ) }

将完全相同，只是一个具有两个输入的片段作为子数组：

 [ { type: Input }, { type: Input }, ]

那么，为什么在一种情况下我们需要一个“钥匙”来让 React 运行，而在另一种情况下 - 不需要？

不同之处在于第一个示例是一个动态数组。 因此，它迫使您添加“密钥”作为预防措施：以防您在运行中弄乱阵列。

你可能会问这里的乐趣在哪里？ 就是这样：尝试渲染那些不在具有相同“键”的数组中的输入，有条件地应用：

 const Component = () => { const [isReverse, setIsReverse] = useState(false); // no-one cares about "key" here return ( <> <Input key={isReverse ? 'some-key' : null} /> <Input key={!isReverse ? 'some-key' : null} /> </> ) }

尝试预测如果我在这些输入中键入内容并打开和关闭布尔值会发生什么。 我会把它留给你做作业😊

在 codesandbox 中检查代码

如果您仔细阅读了整篇文章，您现在可能会轻微心脏病发作。 当我调查所有这些时，我当然有一个。 因为…

  • 如果将动态项目转换为子数组，这与粘在一起的普通元素没有什么不同
  • 如果我将普通项目放在动态数组之后
  • 并添加或删除数组中的项目

这是否意味着这个数组之后的项目总是会重新安装自己？？ 因为如果将其转换为包含三个孩子的数组 - 前两个是动态的，最后一个是静态的 - 它将是。 如果是这种情况，那么定义对象将是这样的：

 [ { type: Input, key: 1 }, // input from the array { type: Input, key: 2 }, // input from the array { type: Input }, // input after the array ]

如果我向data数组中添加另一个项目，则在第三个位置将有一个Input元素，其中key="3"来自数组，并且“手动”输入将移动到第四个位置，这意味着从从 React 的角度来看，这是一个需要安装的新项目。

幸运的是，情况并非如此。 当我们混合动态和静态元素时，就像上面的代码一样，React 只是创建一个包含这些动态元素的数组，并使整个数组成为子元素数组中的第一个子元素。 这将是该代码的定义对象：

 [ // the entire dynamic array is the first position in the children's array [{ type: Input, key: 1 }, { type: Input, key: 2 }], { type: Input // this is our manual Input after the array } ]

我们的手动Input将始终排在第二位。 不会有重新安装。 没有性能灾难。 心脏病发作是不必要的。

关于协调的最后一件有趣的事情在这里值得一提：我们可以对为什么在其他组件内部声明组件是一种反模式有一个明确的答案。

即，当我们这样做时：

 const Component = () => { const Input = () => <input />; return <Input /> }

重渲染重装的大神们会很不爽的。 如果父Component出于任何原因重新呈现自身，则内部声明的Input组件将在每次重新呈现时重新安装自身。 性能很糟糕，有很多潜在的错误。

如果我们从协调和定义对象的角度来看这段代码，这就是我们在Component中所拥有的：

只是一个具有指向函数的“类型”属性的对象。 但是，该函数是在Component内部创建的。 它是该组件的本地组件，因此每次重新渲染时都会重新创建。 因此，当 React 尝试比较这些类型时，它会比较两个不同的函数：一个在重新渲染之前，一个在重新渲染之后。 你不能比较 JavaScript 中的函数，不是这样的。

 const a = () => {}; const b = () => {}; a === b; // will always be false

结果，那个孩子的“类型”在每次重新渲染时都会不同，因此 React 将删除“前一个”组件并安装“下一个”组件。 因此重新安装。

这就是今天的全部内容，希望从现在开始您将只看到对象而不是 React 元素，并且可以通过查看它们在渲染输出中的位置来预测哪个组件将重新渲染以及哪个组件将重新安装。 下次有人半夜叫醒你问：“为什么我们在 React 中需要‘钥匙’？” 您将能够击中他们的眼睛以得出一个好的答案。 不过，您的同事可能会开始将您用作重新渲染的调试工具，所以请注意这一点。
